title: haplopappus breviradiatus
intro: Haplopappus breviradiatus, also known as dwarf greasewood or low sagebrush, is a shrub-like plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpenes and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of Haplopappus breviradiatus have been found to provide relief from various health issues, including sore throats, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, and are often used to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
